content:
Assertion : Tert-butyl methyl ether react with $$\mathrm{HBr}$$ to form tert butyl, $$\left(\mathrm{CH}_3\right)_3 \mathrm{C}-\mathrm{Br}$$ and $$\mathrm{CH}_3-\mathrm{OH}$$, methanal.

Reason : It follows $$\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 1$$ mechanism.

options.content: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion.
If both assertion and reason are true, but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion.
If assertion is true, but reason is false.
If both assertion and reason are false.

explanation:
Ethers are readily cleaved by halogen acids at $$373 \mathrm{~K}$$ to form an alcohol and an alkyl halide.

$$\underset{\text { Tert-butyl methylether }}{\left(\mathrm{CH}_3\right)_3 \mathrm{COCH}_3}+\mathrm{HBr} \longrightarrow \underset{\text { Tert-butyl bromide }}{\left(\mathrm{CH}_3\right)_3 \mathrm{CBr}}+\underset{\text { Methanol }}{\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{OH}}$$

$$\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 1$$ mechanism is followed as $$(\mathrm{CH}_3)_3 \mathrm{C}^{+}(s)$$ is most stable carbocation and $$\mathrm{I}^{-}$$ is attached to it.

content:
Assertion : Phenol reacts with $$\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{I}$$ in presence of $$\mathrm{NaOH}$$ to form methoxybenzene.

Reason : Phenoxide is better nucleophile than phenol.

options.content: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion.
If both assertion and reason are true, but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion.
If assertion is true, but reason is false.
If both assertion and reason are false.

explanation:
When phenol reacts with $$\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{I}$$ in presence of $$\mathrm{NaOH}$$, it gives methoxybenzene due to formation of phenoxide ion, which is a better nucleophile than phenol.

The reaction takes place as follows :

Thus given assertion and reason, both are correct and given reason is the correct answer of the given assertion.

Hence, option (a) is the correct.

content:
Assertion (A) IUPAC name of the compound

Reason (R) In IUPAC nomenclature, ether is regarded as hydrocarbon derivative in which a hydrogen atom is replaced by-OR or- $$\mathrm{O} A r$$ group [where, $$R=$$ alkyl group and $$A r=\operatorname{aryl}$$ group].

options.content: Both (A) and (R) are true and (R) is the correct explanation of (A).
Both (A) and (R) are true, but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A).

explanation:
IUPAC name of the above given compound is correct, i.e. 1-(2-propoxy) propane. In this compound, hydrogen atom is replaced by—OR group and thus named ether.

Therefore, both assertion and reason is correct and reason is the correct explanation of the assertion.

content:
Assertion (A) $$\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{OH}$$ and $$\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{OH}$$ can be distinguished by haloform test.

Reason (R) Haloform test is given by $$2^{\circ}$$ alcohol.

options.content: Both (A) and (R) are true and (R) is the correct explanation of (A).
Both (A) and (R) are true, but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A).

explanation:
Haloform test is used to identify the compound (alcohol or carbonyl compound). In this reaction, alcohols (aldehydes and ketones) on heating with $$X_2\left(\mathrm{Cl}_2, \mathrm{Br}_2\right.$$ and $$\left.\mathrm{I}_2\right)$$ and $$\mathrm{OH}^{-}$$ form $$\mathrm{CH} X_3$$ (haloform). This test is given by $$\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{OH}$$,

Therefore, both assertion and reason is correct but reason in not the correct explanation of the assertion.
